metadata.title: CX Imperatives 2026
metadata.subtitle: Consumer Survey – Interactive Data Explorer
metadata.description: Merkle's annual consumer experience research covering 2,500 respondents across 30 countries on CX expectations, personalization, and AI.

questions.text: First, what is your current age?

responses.label: Gen Z
Millennial
Gen X
Baby Boomer

questions.text: In which country are you located?

responses.label: United States (USA)
Canada
Mexico
Brazil
Argentina
Colombia

questions.text: Region

responses.label: NAM
LATAM
EUR
APAC

questions.text: Including yourself and any minors (under age 18), how many people live in your household?

responses.label: 1 (I live alone)
7 or more

questions.text: Are you responsible for any children (under 18) living in your household?

responses.label: Yes
No
Prefer not to say

questions.text: In which of the following ranges is your personal net worth - i.e., the combined value of all of your cash, bank accounts, and investments (e.g., stocks, bonds, mutual funds, retirement accounts)?

responses.label: Less than USD $50,000
USD $1 million to less than $5 million
USD $5 million to less than $10 million
USD $10 million or more
Prefer not to say

questions.text: HNWI

responses.label: HNWI
NON-HNWI

questions.text: Which of the following best describes the local area or community where you live?

responses.label: Urban (densely populated area inside a city)
Suburban (moderately populated area near a city, but not inside it)
Rural (sparsely populated area not near a city - i.e., countryside)
Prefer not to say

questions.text: Which of the following best describes your residential status (for your primary residence)?

responses.label: Owner (I own my primary residence)
Renter (I rent or lease my primary residence)
Other (I neither own nor rent / lease my primary residence)
Prefer not to say

questions.text: Which of the following actions have you taken in the past three months, if any?

responses.label: Made a purchase (either in-person or online)
Shopped at a store (or visited a physical location / office)
Shopped on a website or mobile app
Used / consumed a product or service
Contacted customer service or requested support
Joined or participated in a rewards / loyalty program
Started / renewed a subscription or service plan
Donated goods or money
None of the Above

questions.text: Within the past three months, which of the following activities or interactions have you experienced when engaging with a company or brand?

responses.label: Received a promotional offer or discount
Redeemed customer loyalty points for a reward
Received an update (e.g., on a transaction, account activity, etc.)
Used a virtual assistant or chatbot for more information
Provided customer feedback through a survey or review
Requested a refund for a product or service
Canceled my account or subscription
None of the above

questions.text: You indicated that you engage within the past three months. With which type of company or brand did you have this interaction?
crossTabColumns: Shopped at a store (or visited a physical location / office)
Shopped on a website or mobile app
Made a purchase (either in-person or online)
Used / consumed a product or service
Started / renewed a subscription or service plan
Joined or participated in a rewards / loyalty program
Contacted customer service or requested support

responses.label: Retailer (that sells products directly to consumers through its stores, apps, or websites)
Packaged goods brand (that makes small, consumable household products - e.g., foods, drinks, cosmetics, over-the-counter medicines, personal hygiene products, cleaning supplies, pet supplies, etc.)
Clothing or textile brand (that makes apparel, shoes, fashion accessories, rugs, curtains, linens, towels, etc.)
Financial services provider (that helps consumers manage their savings and investments, such as a bank, credit card company, loan provider, financial advisor, investment broker, etc.)
Insurance provider (that provides insurance plans / coverage for consumers - e.g., health , dental, vision, life, pet, home, auto, etc.)
Healthcare provider (that provides health services to consumers, such as a doctor, dentist, health clinic, pharmacist, psychiatrist, etc.)
Household (durable) goods brand (that makes large, durable household items and equipment, such as furniture, appliances, storage containers, lights & fixtures, etc.)
Outdoor recreation or sporting goods brand (that makes products for outdoor recreation or sports, such as camping gear, kayaks / canoes, fishing and hunting supplies, football gear, etc.)
Vehicle manufacturer (that makes vehicles, such as cars, boats, bicycles, motorcycles, scooters, etc.)
Electronics (hardware) brand (that makes consumer hardware, such as computers, TVs, speakers, phones, etc.)
Software brand (that makes consumer software, such as computer programs, smartphone apps, video games, computer operating systems, etc.)
Telecommunications service provider (that offers telecom plans / services to consumers, such as internet service, phone service, cable television service, satellite TV or radio service, etc.)
Travel or transportation company (such as an airline, travel agency, taxi service, railroad / train service, etc.)
Restaurant or foodservice brand (such as a fast food or sit-down restaurant, bar, catering service, food delivery service, etc.)
Hospitality brand (such as a hotel, inn, hostel, vacation rental company, etc.)
Media brand (that makes books, magazines, television shows, video games, etc.)
Entertainment brand (such as a theme park, concert hall, sports arena, theater, etc.)
Nonprofit organization (such as a school, church, charity group, political advocacy group, etc.)
None of the above

questions.text: Please think about all the times you have purchased from / donated to a your assigned brand category over the past 2-3 years. Which of the following channels have you used?
crossTabColumns: outdoor recreation or sporting goods brand

responses.label: Physical ("brick-and-mortar") store or location
Brand website
Brand mobile app
Third-party online marketplace
Social media website or app (such as Instagram, X, Pinterest, etc.)
Voice-enabled smart speaker / home device (e.g., Amazon Alexa)
TV streaming site / app (e.g., shoppable ads, in-platform purchasing, live shopping streams, etc.)
None of the above

questions.text: How important is it for the experience to be... (Top 2 Box: Very/Extremely Important)

activities.activity: SHOP (in store or online)

responses.label: Safe and secure (e.g., protecting my data / privacy / identity)
Easy / convenient
Clear and simple (without a lot of steps, details, or options)
Reliable or consistent every time
Reflective of my personal values and beliefs
Personal or human in nature
Affordable
Emotionally inspiring/uplifting (makes me feel something, improves my mood, etc.)
Personalized or customized to me
Fun / entertaining
Immersive (i.e., had stimulating visuals, audio, scents, flavors, etc.)
Powered or enhanced by new technology, like AI

activities.activity: SHOPPED (in store or online)

responses.label: experience was frictionless (fast and easy)
experience felt empowering or emotionally satisfying
brand showed that it values my long-term business / loyalty as a customer
brand understood and anticipated what I wanted or needed during this experience
experience felt personalized or customized to me, individually
brand showed me media content and information that was relevant and helpful

questions.text: Reflecting on your experiences and interactions with brands, what types of personalization do you value the most?
crossTabColumns: outdoor recreation or sporting goods brand

responses.label: Personalized discounts or promotional offers
Personalized loyalty rewards (e.g., birthday perks, exclusive experiences, unique missions or challenges, etc.)
Personalized product recommendations
Personalized communications (emails, texts, etc.)
Personalized products or services (monogrammed items, services catering to your preferences, etc.)
Location-based experiences (e.g., app-based offers when you enter a physical store)
Personalized media content (images, video, audio)
Personalized interfaces / layouts (e.g., brand websites or apps organized to your preferences)
Personalized advertisements
Other types of personalization (specify)
None of the above

questions.text: Which of the following best describes how you typically feel when brands use your personal data to personalize or customize your experiences and interactions?

crossTabColumns: outdoor recreation or sporting goods brand

responses.label: NET - TOP 2 BOX [Like it / okay with it]
I like it - it makes my experience better
I'm generally okay with it as long as my data is secure
I'm generally uncomfortable with it - it feels a bit creepy or intrusive
I dislike it a lot and try to avoid brands that do this
NET - BOTTOM 2 BOX [Uncomfortable / dislike it]
Unsure/not applicable
NET - TOP 2 BOX [Like it / okay with it]
I like it - it makes my experience better
I'm generally okay with it as long as my data is secure
I'm generally uncomfortable with it - it feels a bit creepy or intrusive
I dislike it a lot and try to avoid brands that do this
NET - BOTTOM 2 BOX [Uncomfortable / dislike it]
Unsure/not applicable

questions.text: How do you generally feel about the amount of personalization that you are receiving from brands?

crossTabColumns: outdoor recreation or sporting goods brand

responses.label: There isn't enough personalization
There's the right amount personalization
There's too much personalization
Unsure/not applicable
There isn't enough personalization
There's the right amount personalization
There's too much personalization
Unsure/not applicable

questions.text: [TOP 2 BOX - Very / Extremely Helpful] And how do you generally feel about the helpfulness or usefulness of personalized content (product / service recommendations, ads, emails, etc.) you are receiving from brands?

crossTabColumns: outdoor recreation or sporting goods brand

responses.label: Personalized content is extremely helpful
Personalized content is very helpful
Personalized content is moderately helpful
Personalized content is slightly helpful
Personalized content is not at all helpful
Unsure/not applicable
Personalized content is extremely helpful
Personalized content is very helpful
Personalized content is moderately helpful
Personalized content is slightly helpful
Personalized content is not at all helpful
Unsure/not applicable

questions.text: [TOP 2 BOX - Somewhat / Strongly Agree] To what extent do you agree or disagree with the following statements about personalization, overall?)

responses.label: I prefer to control the level of personalization I receive
Personalization makes me feel valued as a customer
I expect personalization to be a standard part of customer experiences
It's frustrating when personalized suggestions miss the mark
Too much personalization makes it challenging to discover new things

questions.text: [TOP 2 BOX - Somewhat / Strongly Agree] How much do you agree or disagree with the following statements about "high-touch" brand experiences?

responses.label: Good products/services speak for themselves - they don't need fancy treatment.
I feel a stronger emotional connection to brands that create experiences that are personal and human.
Brands that pay attention to small details are worth paying extra for.
High-touch service makes me feel more confident in my purchase decisions.
When brands design products and packaging around personalization, they care more about their customers.
Brands that try too hard to be personal come off as inauthentic.

questions.text: Which of the following features would make you feel the most valued by a brand?

responses.label: Personalized discounts or promo offers (e.g., birthday coupon, lifetime customer discount, etc.)
Early access to new products, exclusive events, or special discounts
Consistent experiences across channels (e.g., mobile, in-store, online)
Thoughtful human touches (e.g., handwritten notes)
Conversational communication (like I'm talking to a friend)
Thoughtful digital features (e.g., tailored recommendations, intuitive design)
Premium elements (e.g., elegant typography, high quality materials)
Personalized packaging or messaging (e.g., monogramming)
Sensory elements (e.g., signature scents, textures, sounds)
None of the above

questions.text: [FAMILIARITY - Somewhat / Very Familiar] Below is a list of technology-enabled ways that you can engage with brands as a consumer. How familiar are you with each one?

responses.label: Brand / Company Mobile Apps
Self-Checkout / Self-Scanning (in physical stores)
Personalized Emails / Text Messages
Generative AI (GenAI) (e.g., ChatGPT, Google Gemini, DALL-E)
Personalized Recommendations / Offers
AI Chatbot (e.g, online chat for simple questions/answers)
Smart Home Devices
Visual Search / Image Search
AI Assistant / Virtual Assistant (e.g., smart helper like Alexa, Google or Siri)
Personalized Advertisements
Shoppable Social Media Content or Social Commerce
Shoppable TV / Video Streaming Content
Digital Avatars / Virtual Avatars (digital, animated versions of your face / identity, for instance in chat messages)
AI Video Chat (e.g., live video conversation with an AI assistant)
Gamified / Game-Like Experiences (e.g., challenges or missions to win rewards, points, or prizes)
AI Agents / Agentic AI (AI programs that can independently act on your behalf to complete more complex customer interactions, like resolving billing problems or finding products you might like)
Metaverse / Virtual Reality (VR) (e.g., virtual stores / shopping experiences)
Augmented Reality / AR (e.g., 'try it on' feature)
Voice-Enabled Purchases (i.e., making a purchase vocally via a smartphone, smart home speaker, etc.)

questions.text: USAGE [Use rarely / occasionally / often / always] Now, how often would you say you use or interact with each of these technologies, when you are engaging with your assigned brand categorys as a customer (i.e., visiting brand websites / apps, shopping in stores, requesting service or support, etc.)?
